top_desired_outcomes.signal: QA to be integrated throughout the entire development lifecycle, contributing early.
QA to be valued, respected, and paid commensurate with developers.

Clear, solid requirements and acceptance criteria provided to QA.

Having clear criteria and processes for quality gates that are supported by management

evidence: Review 3: 'The testing that was most important for that product was also just good old fashioned human judgement.'
Review 6: 'I gave it context of another test that I had written a long time ago which I knew was the gold standard.'
Review 6: 'our APIs are all similiar, they more or less take the database table schema as input' indicates deep understanding of internal system structures and data flows.
Review 6: 'my company has invest a lot of money in AI and I believe our models have been trained against our codebase.' shows awareness of internal compliance and training systems.
Review 3: 'It's just a website same as any other website. No different testing needed.' suggests a preference for stable, predictable systems.
Review 6: 'I gave it context of another test that I had written a long time ago which I knew was the gold standard.' indicates a focus on proven, maintainable solutions over novel but risky ones.

signals.signal_name: Skepticism of Novelty for Novelty's Sake
evidence: Review 3: 'It's just a website same as any other website. No different testing needed.'
Review 6: 'I gave it context of another test that I had written a long time ago which I knew was the gold standard.'
Review 6: 'I gave it context of another test that I had written a long time ago which I knew was the gold standard.'
Review 6: 'It had the best results out of llama & openai.'
Review 6: 'I gave it context of another test that I had written a long time ago which I knew was the gold standard.'
Review 6: 'It did a really good job, I had to make minor tweaks but was able to make a merge request out of it.'

Review 1: 'As an amateur scholar of Yiddishkeit, my readings have included several novels and biographies set in the Warsaw Ghetto, so I was familiar with the horrific overcrowding and dehumanizing conditions...'

Review 2: 'As an amateur scholar of Yiddishkeit, my readings have included several novels and biographies set in the Warsaw Ghetto, so I was familiar with the horrific overcrowding and dehumanizing conditions...'

Review 1: 'At times, the novel is nearly bogged down by the overenthusiastic descriptions, such as a segment on beetles that goes on for several pages, but these scenic detours serve to illuminate the thinking behind several pivotal characters.'

Review 1: 'There are certainly important concepts glossed over, such as the Hasidic viewpoint of the Shoah, and at times the quotes taken from Antonina's diary and other documents blur between fiction and recounting based on the sparse endnotes...'
Review 1: '...at times the quotes taken from Antonina's diary and other documents blur between fiction and recounting based on the sparse endnotes...'
Review 3: 'Yeah. And, would you ever trust AI blindly with any regulation? They don’t.'

signals.signal_name: Value for Comprehensive and Detailed Information
evidence: Review 3: 'Gina DePalma's Dolce Italiano: Desserts from the Babbo Kitchen collects a treasure trove of desserts and savories...'
Review 3: 'She includes a section called Learning Italian that covers various regions, DOP and IGP origins, a recommended reading list, ten Italian ingredients you should know...'

signals.signal_name: Skepticism Towards Overly Simplistic or Unverified Solutions
evidence: Review 3: 'Yeah. And, would you ever trust AI blindly with any regulation? They don’t.'

Another exact quote if needed.

Disillusionment with industry trends that devalue deep expertise, such as outsourcing QA or eliminating QA departments in favor of developer-written tests.
Burnout from working after hours or weekends due to tight schedules and being the 'clean-up crew' for problems that could have been prevented earlier.

top_desired_outcomes.signal: Achieve manageable workload with clear prioritization and realistic expectations
Have time and capacity to perform QA duties properly, including regression testing and ticket handling
Gain respect and proper integration within the product development lifecycle
Automate repetitive tasks to free up time for more valuable, critical-thinking work
Clarify role responsibilities to avoid being tasked with non-QA work (e.g., establishing designs/requirements)
Be involved in planning sessions to understand business logic and product scope, enabling proactive contribution
Prevent problems early in the planning/design phase to reduce drama and rework later in the development cycle.
Have clear, well-defined roles and responsibilities where each function (QA, Dev, BA, PM) can focus on their core expertise.
Implement effective, automated test pipelines (e.g., in CI/CD) that provide fast feedback and prevent broken code from merging, without sacrificing thoroughness.
Maintain product quality and deep understanding of the entire application, even as development pace increases.
Work with clear, comprehensive, and easily accessible information/documentation.
Achieve strategic partnership and respect for QA within the software development lifecycle

top_deal_breakers.signal: Forces role ambiguity or significant scope creep beyond core expertise
Prioritizes speed to market in a way that guarantees significant quality degradation
Lacks clarity, creating persistent friction and inefficiency
Is incomplete or requires lock-in to an unproven series/system
Persistent devaluation or misunderstanding of QA's strategic role by leadership
Products that are fundamentally damaged, misrepresented, or unusable
Resources that are insultingly basic relative to their 15+ years of experience
Requires refactoring of solid existing architecture to work
Promises autonomous solutions that ignore underlying structural issues
Introduces flakiness or false positives into stable processes

top_purchase_triggers.signal: Solves a specific, long-standing need or fills a gap in their collection/expertise.
Offers high utility, practical advice, or actionable knowledge aligned with their professional or personal interests.
Matches established, trusted preferences (author, series, publisher, theme).
Facilitates a meaningful personal or shared experience (e.g., bonding, education, tradition).
Delivers exceptional quality in content, presentation, or craftsmanship.
Need to update or refresh deep domain knowledge after time away from the field
Seeking to enhance a specific, established skill within a familiar system
Desire for a reliable desktop reference for specific, on-demand information
